# Patterns excluded from `rsync --delete` deploys.
#
# These patterns serve two purposes:
#   1. Files matched here are NOT transferred from the runner.
#   2. Files matched here are NOT removed on the destination by --delete.
#
# Anything that should live only on the server (env files, runtime
# logs, server-managed state) MUST be listed here so it isn't wiped
# on deploy.

# --- VCS / CI / agent metadata ---
.git/
.github/
.gitignore
.dockerignore
.serena/
.serena.local.bak/
.semgrep/
.superpowers/
.worktrees/
.claude/

# --- IDE / local tooling ---
.vscode/
.idea/
.phpcs.cache

# --- Tests, dev-only sources ---
e2e/
docs/
playwright.config.ts
playwright-report/
test-results/

# --- Build / dev configuration with no runtime role ---
phpcs.xml
.markdownlint.yml
Dockerfile

# --- Node / Bun (only used for E2E tests + markdown linting) ---
node_modules/
package.json
bun.lock

# --- Project meta files ---
CLAUDE.md
CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
README.md

# --- TRACKED EXAMPLE FILE (transferred + overwritten every deploy) ---
# The "+" prefix is an rsync include-override: this rule must precede the
# matching ".env.*" exclude below for first-match-wins to honor it. We
# intentionally DO want .env.example to ship and overwrite the server's
# copy on every deploy so admins can diff it against .env.production to
# spot new or deprecated environment variables.
+ .env.example

# --- SERVER-MANAGED FILES (never transferred, never --delete) ---
# .env / .env.production carry the live secrets; logs/ accumulates
# runtime logs. Both stay untouched by rsync.
.env
.env.*
/logs/*

# --- RUNTIME TEST-RUN RESULTS (preserve server-side state) ---
# results/*.json are stored test runs written by results.php at runtime.
# Excludes contents only; the directory itself ships (it is tracked via
# .gitkeep locally but .gitkeep matches this pattern, so the dir is also
# created in a pre-rsync SSH-exec step).
/results/*

# --- DEPLOY RESTART SENTINEL (server-managed) ---
# After rsync, the deploy drops tmp/restart.txt for a root-side php-fpm
# reload watcher (see deploy.yaml). Exclude the contents so --delete never
# races the sentinel; the dir is created pre-rsync.
/tmp/*
